name: ASIAN-INSPIRED SWEET CHILI TOFU AND PINEAPPLE RICE
intro: When I first started my vegan journey, I ate a lot of Thai and other Asian dishes because a lot of them are made without animal products, and it was a quick and easy vegan fix for me. Growing up in a diverse neighborhood, I had a lot of access to different types of cuisines. Even though I grew up in the Bronx, we still had the privilege of trying many different foods from many different cultures. And it has influenced the palate that I have today. For this recipe, it’s important to squeeze out as much moisture as you can from the tofu. This will help firm up the tofu for the frying process, so it tastes as meaty as possible. If you don’t want to use tofu, feel free to use cauliflower! It tastes just as good.

ingredients.item: frozen mixed veggies (peas, carrots, corn, string beans, etc.)
cooked white or brown rice, cold
soy sauce or liquid amino acids

method: In a large wok or nonstick pan over medium heat, cook your JUST Egg, scrambling it for about 3 minutes, or until crumbles form, then remove it from the wok.

Add your sesame oil, chile (if using), garlic, onion and mixed veggies, and cook for about 5 minutes. Toss in your cold cooked rice (make sure it isn’t too moist; you can even use rice left over from the day before and stored in the refrigerator).
Add your salt, pepper, soy sauce, cashews and egg, Toss and mix for about 7 minutes, then mix in your pineapple and cook for 2 more minutes; then, you’re done.
In a large bowl, mix together 2 cups (250 g) of flour, salt, pepper, garlic powder and paprika; this is your dry batter.
In another bowl, mix together the remaining cup (125 g) of flour, hot sauce and nondairy milk. You should have a batterlike consistency; this is your wet batter.

In a large pot, heat the oil to 350°F (180°C). Drain your tofu and gently squeeze out all the water. You can put it inside a clean towel to do this. With your hands, gently rip apart the tofu into bite-sized chunks and dredge them in the dry batter. Once coated, dip the pieces into the wet batter, then into the dry batter again. Working in batches, add the tofu pieces to the oil and fry until they turn golden brown. Transfer the fried tofu to a bowl. Add your sweet chili sauce and mix until the fried tofu is coated.
Fill half of your plate or bowl with the pineapple rice and the tofu. Garnish with green onion and sesame seeds.
